2008-11-01.title: Georgia’s Battered Face in a Separatist Republic
2008-11-01.abstract: For 18 months, Dmitri Sanakoev was the public face of Georgia’s “hearts and minds” campaign, president of an alternative government for South Ossetia.

2008-11-01.title: Despite Crisis, Wealthy Russians Are Buying Up Coastal Montenegro
2008-11-01.abstract: Thanks in large part to wealthy Russians, Montenegro has received more foreign investment per capita than any other country on the Continent.

2008-11-01.title: Libya Pays to End Terrorism Cases
2008-11-01.abstract: Libya deposited $1.5 billion with the State Department to be shared among more than 400 victims, mostly American, for terrorist attacks in the mid-1980s.

2008-11-01.title: A Warning, a Blast, a Fight to Save an Afghan Life
2008-11-01.abstract: In an isolated Afghan outpost, it was up to an American Army doctor and medics to keep a wounded Afghan alive.

2008-11-01.title: Ukraine: Bid for Loan Supported
2008-11-01.abstract: The Parliament passed legislation aimed at securing a $16.5 billion International Monetary Fund loan to shore up the country’s finances.

2008-11-02.title: A thunder clap, two downpours and a perfect end to the F1 season
2008-11-02.abstract: One hour and four minutes before the Brazilian Grand Prix started in Sao Paulo today everyone in the paddock, and all the spectators, suddenly raised their heads after hearing a message from the heavens: The most extraordinary crack of thunder exploded over the circuit, the first such sound of the day.

2008-11-02.title: More Kim Jong-Il Photos Are Released
2008-11-02.abstract: The North Korean state-run news media released photos showing its leader smiling and looking vivacious, some two months after he was believed to have suffered a stroke.

2008-11-02.title: Chinese Regulators Destroy Tons of Tainted Animal Feed
2008-11-02.abstract: Officials said that they had confiscated and destroyed more than 3,600 tons of animal feed tainted with melamine.

2008-11-02.title: In City of Tolerance, Shadow of the Taliban
2008-11-02.abstract: In recent weeks, panic has found its way to Lahore, Pakistan, a city long regarded as the cultural, intellectual and artistic heart of the country.

2008-11-03.title: Aid Group Says Zimbabwe Misused $7.3 Million
2008-11-03.abstract: The government of President Robert Mugabe is accused of misspending funds donated to fight killer diseases.

2008-11-03.title: Israel Acts to Cut Off Funds to Illegal Settlements
2008-11-03.abstract: The decision is one of a series of measures announced by Prime Minister Ehud Olmert in response to a rise in violence by extremist Jewish settlers in the West Bank.

2008-11-03.title: A Bomb Kills 8 Pakistanis, and It Is Seen as a Warning
2008-11-03.abstract: A suicide bomber rammed a car into a security checkpoint in the South Waziristan tribal region on Sunday, killing eight soldiers, officials said.

2008-11-03.title: Congo on the Brink
2008-11-03.abstract: Congo, at the green heart of the continent, never goes down alone. The worry now is that Congo is teetering again, with neighbors poised to jump in.

2008-11-03.title: Russians With Pumpkins Protest Many U.S. Plots
2008-11-03.abstract: Thousands gathered in front of the U.S. Embassy in Moscow carrying jack-o’-lanterns inked with the name of war victims, charging that the war in Georgia was one of many American plots.

2008-11-04.title: Conviction in Spy Case Over Cash-Filled Suitcase
2008-11-04.abstract: A jury in Miami convicted a Venezuelan businessman of acting as an “unregistered agent” of Venezuela on American soil.

2008-11-04.title: That Roar in the Jungle Is 15,000 Motorbikes
2008-11-04.abstract: Tabatinga owes much of its moto-obsession to its location along Brazil’s extreme western frontier, where it has evolved into a hub of cross-border commerce.

2008-11-04.title: European Union to Be Led by Former Soviet Satellite
2008-11-04.abstract: The Czech Republic assumes the rotating presidency of the European Union, the first former Soviet bloc country to lead the group of 495 million Europeans.

2008-11-04.title: Finance Chiefs of Europe Reject Package for Stimulus
2008-11-04.abstract: European finance ministers vowed instead to coordinate national policies as they try to limit the fallout from a recession on consumers and companies.

2008-11-04.title: As Ukraine Staggers, Its Leaders Quarrel
2008-11-04.abstract: Confronted by the financial crisis, Ukraine’s leaders are locked in a power struggle that has paralyzed the state.

2008-11-05.title: Analysis from the Street: Black and Blue State
2008-11-05.abstract: Six thousand miles away from the White House on the streets of Baghdad, the American election is overwhelmingly seen through the prism of one, single and immediate issue far closer to home. Whether President Bush’s successor should remove the American troops and when.

2008-11-05.title: Urban Cowboys Struggle With India’s Sacred Strays
2008-11-05.abstract: “Cow catchers” spend their days roping cattle on the streets of India’s capital as part of a long and frustrating battle to rid it of stray cows.

2008-11-05.title: Mexico’s Interior Minister Killed in Jet Crash
2008-11-05.abstract: The minister, Juan Camilo Mouriño, and seven others were killed in Mexico City.

2008-11-05.title: Colombian Army Commander Resigns in Scandal Over Killing of Civilians
2008-11-05.abstract: An investigation tied military personnel under Gen. Mario Montoya’s command to the killing of civilians in attempts to inflate the number of insurgents.

2008-11-05.title: Israeli Strike Is First in Gaza Since Start of Cease-Fire
2008-11-05.abstract: Israel launched an airstrike on Gaza after its troops clashed with Hamas gunmen along the border. Five militants were killed, Palestinian officials said.

2008-11-06.title: Seeking Majority, Quebec’s Premier Sets Election
2008-11-06.abstract: Jean Charest, the premier of Quebec, announced an early election for the province on Wednesday, less than a month after a federal election in Canada.

2008-11-06.title: In German Hearts, a Pirate Spreads the Plunder Again
2008-11-06.abstract: The legend of Störtebeker, the egalitarian pirate, has been rejuvenated in Germany, partly as a response to the growing sense of economic justice.

2008-11-06.title: U.S. Envoy Hosts First Event at New Embassy in Baghdad
2008-11-06.abstract: Ambassador Ryan C. Crocker welcomed about 250 Iraqi officials, diplomats and dignitaries for a preliminary glimpse of the vast new American Embassy in Baghdad on Wednesday morning.

2008-11-06.title: Malaysian Blogger Fights a System He Perfected
2008-11-06.abstract: Mahathir Mohamad, former prime minister of Malaysia, is one of an increasing number of bloggers challenging government censorship and gaining influence in the political process.

2008-11-06.title: Zimbabwe: Female Protest Leaders Are Granted Bail
2008-11-06.abstract: Jenni Williams and Magodonga Mahlangu, leaders of Women of Zimbabwe Arise, have been granted bail by a High Court judge in Bulawayo on a public disorder charge.

2008-11-07.title: A Low Country Seeks Higher Ground
2008-11-07.abstract: A government commission proposed the construction of islands off the Dutch coast in the shape of plants, specifically tulips.

2008-11-07.title: Georgia Claims on Russia War Called Into Question
2008-11-07.abstract: Newly available accounts raise questions about the accuracy and honesty of Georgia’s insistence that it acted defensively against Russian aggression.

2008-11-07.title: Catholics and Muslims Pledge to Improve Links
2008-11-07.abstract: The meeting came a year after Muslim leaders wrote a letter to Pope Benedict XVI after he offended many Muslims with a sermon that contained an anti-Islam quote.

2008-11-07.title: Obama Victory Alters the Tenor of Iraqi Politics
2008-11-07.abstract: Iraqi Shiite politicians are indicating that they will move faster toward a security agreement about U.S. troops.

2008-11-07.title: Governments on Both Sides of the Atlantic Push to Get Banks to Lend
2008-11-07.abstract: Having been brought low by their dealings in obscure securities, bankers are reluctant to start lending again until the impact of the recession becomes clearer.

2008-11-08.title: Son Finds Veil on Father’s Death Under Stalin Lifting a Bit
2008-11-08.abstract: Robin Oggins’s urge to know a fuller account of the redacted chapters of his father’s life propels him toward the center of a lingering cold war mystery.

2008-11-08.title: Protecting Herself as Much as Her Drug Lord Clients
2008-11-08.abstract: Silvia Raquenel Villanueva, Mexico’s most prominent “narco abogada,” or lawyer to the drug lords, continues to receive threats, which she deflects with prayer.

2008-11-08.title: Russia Aims to Be High on Obama’s Agenda
2008-11-08.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev of Russia has thrown down a gauntlet intended to demonstrate to the American president-elect that the post-cold-war era may not be so post after all.

2008-11-08.title: Jihadi Leader Says Radicals Share Obama Victory
2008-11-08.abstract: Abu Omar al-Baghdadi, leader of the Islamic State of Iraq, said that the election of Barack Obama represented a victory for radical Islamic groups.

2008-11-08.title: Georgian Church Envoys Meet With Russian Official
2008-11-08.abstract: The meeting was the first official contacts between Georgia and Russia since war broke out in the breakaway region of South Ossetia in August.

2008-11-09.title: China Unveils Sweeping Plan for Economy
2008-11-09.abstract: China announced plans to spend billions on a wide array of infrastructure and social welfare projects in a bold move aimed at bolstering its economy.

2008-11-09.title: Russian Sub Returns to Base After Accident That Killed 20
2008-11-09.abstract: A brand new Russian nuclear submarine returned to base on Sunday after an accident that killed 20 people and injured 21.

2008-11-09.title: Ossetians Tighten Hold on Village
2008-11-09.abstract: South Ossetian forces on Sunday began reinforcing a border in the village of Perevi, an ethnically Georgian area that has been absorbed into South Ossetian territory.

2008-11-10.title: Finance Chill Freezes Plans for High-Rise in Russia
2008-11-10.abstract: Plans for a controversial skyscraper that would have towered over St. Petersburg’s low-slung Baroque skyline have been delayed because of the financial crisis.

2008-11-10.title: African Leaders Act to Defuse Conflict in Congo
2008-11-10.abstract: The Southern African Development Community called for a cease-fire and safe supply corridors in eastern Congo.

2008-11-10.title: Anger Erupts After Executions in Bali Blasts
2008-11-10.abstract: Hundreds demonstrated after the executions of three men for their roles in the bombings that killed 202 people.

2008-11-10.title: Middle East Negotiators Press Pursuit of a 2-State Solution
2008-11-10.abstract: Middle East peace mediators reaffirmed their backing for Israeli-Palestinian efforts toward a two-state solution, but shed no light on their political progress after a year of negotiations.

2008-11-10.title: Spate of Attacks Leaves 12 Dead Across Iraq
2008-11-10.abstract: At least 12 Iraqis were killed Sunday in a spate of attacks, many of them in provinces where much-lauded Iraqi-led security operations had taken place recently.

2008-11-11.title: European Union to Resume Russian Partnership Talks
2008-11-11.abstract: In a significant step toward normalizing ties with Moscow, the decision was presented as a rare and bracing show of European unity.

2008-11-11.title: Venezuela Positions Itself as a Salon for the Left
2008-11-11.abstract: Some 200 leftist thinkers from around the world convened in the capital for a few days to discuss transitions toward socialism.

2008-11-11.title: Rescuing Cultures of India, From A to Z
2008-11-11.abstract: An academy in the agrarian countryside tries to preserve a culture by steeping a new generation of villagers in their own quickly disappearing traditions.

2008-11-11.title: Maldives Considers Buying Dry Land if Seas Rise
2008-11-11.abstract: The nation of 1,200 low islands in the Indian Ocean is planning to establish a fund so that it can buy a haven for its citizens should global warming raise sea levels at a dangerous pace.

2008-11-11.title: Pakistanis Mired in Brutal Battle to Oust Taliban
2008-11-11.abstract: In a vital corridor to Afghanistan, the Taliban has proven far more resilient than the Pakistani Army anticipated.

2008-11-12.title: On NATO’s Table, Ukraine and a Test of Russian Ties
2008-11-12.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates will join NATO defense ministers this week to discuss how to bring Ukraine rapidly into the alliance.

2008-11-12.title: Emirates See Fiscal Crisis as Chance to Save Culture
2008-11-12.abstract: Emiratis hope that the global economic downturn will reduce the number of foreigners pouring into their country and give them a chance to reassert their way of life.

2008-11-12.title: Saudi Arabia Seeks U.N. Platform to Promote Pluralism Abroad
2008-11-12.abstract: Human rights groups are crying foul that Saudi Arabia is being given the platform of the U.N. to promote religious tolerance abroad while actively combating it at home.

2008-11-12.title: This Time, Southeast Asia Is Not at Center of the Crisis
2008-11-12.abstract: Lacking the immediacy of the crisis of 1997, the twists and turns of the financial turmoil in the West have been received here like news of a plague several valleys away.

2008-11-12.title: Shortage of Sperm Donors in Britain Prompts Calls for Change
2008-11-12.abstract: The donor shortage may be due in part to a change in the law in 2005, which took away donors’ anonymity.

2008-11-13.title: Russia Seeks Inquiry Into Monitors’ Account of Georgia War
2008-11-13.abstract: Newly available accounts suggest that Georgia attacked the South Ossetian separatist capital of Tskhinvali with indiscriminate artillery and rocket fire on Aug. 7.

2008-11-13.title: A Risky New Film on Ataturk
2008-11-13.abstract: A new documentary film shows Mustafa Kemal Ataturk, the revered and godlike founder of Turkey, as a private person with human weaknesses - a rare and risky endeavor.

2008-11-13.title: Turkey’s Hero, Behind the Bronze Veneer
2008-11-13.abstract: A new documentary dares to take a look at the human side of Turkey’s revered war-hero-turned-statesman Mustafa Kemal Ataturk.

2008-11-13.title: Taiwan: Former President to Be Held, Pending Charges
2008-11-13.abstract: A judge ruled Wednesday that former President Chen Shui-bian of Taiwan be held in jail while prosecutors pursued charges of money laundering and corruption against him.

2008-11-13.title: Canada: Seventh Foot Washes Ashore
2008-11-13.abstract: Another human foot in a sneaker has been found on the coast of British Columbia, the police said Wednesday, making it the seventh to was up in the region since August 2007.

2008-11-14.title: Out of Work in China
2008-11-14.abstract: Migrant workers in Guangdong - the manufacturing hub of China's south - are searching for jobs or heading home in the wake of factory shutdowns.

2008-11-14.title: Settlers Who Long to Leave the West Bank
2008-11-14.abstract: While most settlers vow never to abandon the heart of the Jewish homeland, some say they want to move back to within the pre-1967 borders of Israel.

2008-11-14.title: Death in Bulgaria Exposes Rigors of Fighting Graft
2008-11-14.abstract: Exposing corruption is not a battle for the fainthearted in Bulgaria, where graft has seeped into the fabric of life.

2008-11-14.title: Nearly 2,000 Carrying H.I.V. in Chile Were Not Notified
2008-11-14.abstract: Chile’s health minister said that the country’s public health system and private-sector services failed to notify people that they were infected with H.I.V.

2008-11-14.title: Italy: High Court Allows Removal of Feeding Tube
2008-11-14.abstract: Italy’s highest court ruled that the father of a woman in a persistent vegetative state could remove his daughter’s feeding tube, ending a case that has divided the country.

2008-11-15.title: Two Journalists Wounded in Attempted Abduction in Pakistan
2008-11-15.abstract: After conducting interviews with Taliban fighters in the nearby tribal region of Peshawar, a Japanese journalist and an Afghan colleague were shot and wounded.

2008-11-15.title: U.N.’s Envoy, and Food Aid, Reach Congo
2008-11-15.abstract: Aid workers began feeding tens of thousands of refugees in eastern Congo, as an envoy appointed by the U.N. started talks to try to avert a wider war.

2008-11-15.title: As a Memoirist, a Chilean Diplomat Takes Off the White Gloves
2008-11-15.abstract: Heraldo Muñoz overcame his initial reluctance to write about his past when he realized that General Pinochet had affected an entire generation in Chile.

2008-11-15.title: Mexico Crash Inquiry Points to Pilot Error in Turbulence
2008-11-15.abstract: Authorities said that the pilot of a small government jet that crashed last week, killing Mexico’s interior minister, flew too close to a jumbo jet and lost control of the plane.

2008-11-15.title: Yemen: Claim Made on Embassy Blast
2008-11-15.abstract: The Yemeni branch of Al Qaeda has released a statement claiming responsibility for a suicide bombing outside the American Embassy compound in Sana in September.

2008-11-16.title: Russian Hopes Obama’s Win Will Warm Relations
2008-11-16.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev expressed hope Saturday that President-elect Barack Obama’s election would improve relations that have soured under President Bush.

2008-11-16.title: World Leaders Vow Joint Push to Aid Economy
2008-11-16.abstract: The leaders of 20 countries agreed to work more closely, but put off thornier questions until next year in an early challenge for the Obama administration.

2008-11-16.title: South Korea Joins Lucrative Practice of Inviting Medical Tourists to Its Hospitals
2008-11-16.abstract: Procedures that may cost tens of thousands of dollars in the United States can often be done for one-third or even one-tenth of the cost in Asia, with much shorter waiting times.

2008-11-16.title: Militia Leaves a Georgian Village
2008-11-16.abstract: Ossetian militia fighters withdrew Saturday from Perevi, an ethnic Georgian village that became a flashpoint of tensions in recent days as the militia took control and residents fled in fear.

2008-11-16.title: Pact, Approved in Iraq, Sets Time for U.S. Pullout
2008-11-16.abstract: Iraq’s cabinet approved a security agreement that calls for a full withdrawal of American troops from the country by the end of 2011.

2008-11-17.title: Socialist Party in France Fails to Pick Leader
2008-11-17.abstract: The squabbling Socialist Party was unable to come to an agreement at a party congress that was meant to revive them as a serious challenger to President Nicolas Sarkozy’s administration.

2008-11-17.title: Germany’s Green Party Elects First Ethnic Turk as Leader
2008-11-17.abstract: The party elected Cem Ozdemir, the son of Turkish immigrants, to its top political post, the first time any German party has chosen a leader with an ethnic Turkish background.

2008-11-17.title: Tibetan Exiles Meet to Ponder Strategy
2008-11-17.abstract: The calm in many Tibetan areas of western China could soon crumble, depending on the outcome of a meeting of Tibetan exiles scheduled to begin Monday in India.

2008-11-17.title: Karzai Offers Safe Passage to Taliban Leader if He Agrees to Talks
2008-11-17.abstract: Afghan President Hamid Karzai said he would guarantee the safety of Mullah Muhammad Omar for peace talks.

2008-11-17.title: For More of Mexico’s Wealthy, Cost of Living Includes Guards
2008-11-17.abstract: As drug-related violence spirals out of control, security measures have become a way of life for Mexico’s affluent.

2008-11-18.title: Pollution Has Leveled Off, but the Figures Have Holes, Report Says
2008-11-18.abstract: The United Nations report was released two weeks before the world’s environmental ministers are to meet to discuss ways to curb greenhouse gases.

2008-11-18.title: Queen Sofia Unamused by a Book Quoting Her
2008-11-18.abstract: A new book, “The Queen Up Close,” has given Spaniards an uncomfortably close look at their queen’s conservative views.

2008-11-18.title: Pirates Seize Saudi Tanker Off Kenya
2008-11-18.abstract: The Saudi-owned supertanker loaded with more than $100 million worth of crude oil is the largest ship ever hijacked, according to U.S. Navy officials.

2008-11-18.title: Textbooks on Past Offend South Korea’s Conservatives
2008-11-18.abstract: A widely used textbook’s version of how American and Soviet forces took control of Korea in 1945 has angered conservatives in the country.

2008-11-18.title: Congo Violence Reaches Endangered Mountain Gorillas
2008-11-18.abstract: With the recent violence in eastern Congo, there are no trained rangers now to protect the mountain gorillas in the region.

2008-11-19.title: Britain Grapples With Role for Islamic Justice
2008-11-19.abstract: Conservatives and liberals have denounced Islamic courts as poor substitutes for British jurisprudence.

2008-11-19.title: Pregnant Inmate’s Plight Inspires Plea to Medvedev
2008-11-19.abstract: More than 80,000 people have signed an Internet petition to President Dmitri A. Medvedev demanding the release of a jailed pregnant lawyer.

2008-11-19.title: The Dead Tell a Tale China Doesn’t Care to Listen To
2008-11-19.abstract: The Tarim mummies have become protagonists in a political dispute over who should control the Xinjiang Uighur Autonomous Region.

2008-11-19.title: Iranian Detained in Iraq
2008-11-19.abstract: An Iranian was detained by allied forces at Baghdad International Airport on suspicion of smuggling weapons into Iraq, the U.S. military said.

2008-11-19.title: Corrections
2008-11-19.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article on Monday about the failure of France’s opposition Socialists to pick a new leader at their party congress over the weekend referred incorrectly to one of the three candidates, who is best known as the architect of the 35-hour workweek. The candidate, Martine Aubry, is a woman.

2008-11-20.title: Russia: Trial Is Closed to Media
2008-11-20.abstract: The trial of three men accused of involvement in the killing of the journalist Anna Politkovskaya took a new twist when a judge decided Wednesday to bar the news media.

2008-11-20.title: France: Former Premier Is Charged
2008-11-20.abstract: Dominique de Villepin faces criminal charges of complicity in a supposed smear campaign against Nicolas Sarkozy before he was elected president, a judicial official said Wednesday.

2008-11-20.title: U.S. Urges Merchant Ships to Try Steps to Foil Pirates
2008-11-20.abstract: The commander of American and allied naval forces off the coast of Somalia has begun urging merchant vessels to sail with armed guards on board and to travel only within patrolled lanes.

2008-11-20.title: Germany: Boar-Hunting Increases
2008-11-20.abstract: Hunters killed the third highest number of wild boars in Germany since the end of World War II, the German Hunters’ Association said Wednesday.

2008-11-20.title: Britain: Party Issues Warning After Member List Is Put on Web
2008-11-20.abstract: The far-right British National Party said that it would take court action after names of more than 12,000 party members were posted on the Internet.

2008-11-21.title: South Africa Is Aiming to Ease Dangers of Digging for Gold
2008-11-21.abstract: In post-apartheid South Africa, the welfare of miners — nearly all of them black — is a matter of scrupulous concern.

2008-11-21.title: Shiva, Gangland Style, Behind Burly Guards and Security Cameras
2008-11-21.abstract: When Yaakov Alperon, referred to by many as Israel’s Tony Soprano, was killed, members of his large clan and associates gathered to sit Shiva, the traditional Jewish mourning period.

2008-11-21.title: In Baghdad, Debating Post-U.S. Outlook
2008-11-21.abstract: Some Iraqi lawmakers are worried that the security pact with the U.S. will give too much control to the Shiite-led government of Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki.

2008-11-21.title: Mai Mai Fighters Third Piece in Congo’s Violent Puzzle
2008-11-21.abstract: With their guns and concocted potions, the Mai Mai are a surreal — but still deadly — dimension to Congo’s civil wars.

2008-11-21.title: Russia: Juror Disputes Judge’s Statement on Media
2008-11-21.abstract: The jury in the trial of three men accused in the killing of the journalist Anna Politkovskaya did not demand that journalists be barred from the proceedings, as a judge said a day earlier.

2008-11-22.title: Bush, in Peru, Will Press Plans to Avert Future Financial Crises
2008-11-22.abstract: President Bush, confessing he already “felt a little nostalgic,” began what could be his last official overseas trip on Friday, arriving in Peru for a financial meeting.

2008-11-22.title: Thousands Protest in Baghdad
2008-11-22.abstract: More than 10,000 supporters of the radical Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr gathered in Baghdad on Friday to protest a security agreement with the United States.

2008-11-22.title: Mexico Arrests Ex-Chief of Antidrug Agency
2008-11-22.abstract: Noé Ramírez Mandujano is the highest-ranking official to come under suspicion in a purge of police and prosecutors for possible ties to drug traffickers.

2008-11-22.title: Corrections
2008-11-22.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article on Nov. 14 about a criminal complaint filed by human rights lawyers in the 1989 killing of six Jesuit priests in El Salvador erroneously included two people among those the lawyers accused of having had a role in the murders or in the cover-up that followed. The complaint named the Salvadoran president at the time, Alfredo Cristiani Burkard, who was accused of a cover-up, and 14 former members of the Salvadoran military; it did not implicate two female employees of the priests. The two were victims of the attack and died along with the priests.

2008-11-22.title: Anti-Land Mine Campaign Says Britain Isn’t Abiding by Treaty
2008-11-22.abstract: Nearly 10 years after it signed a treaty banning land mines, Britain had failed even to start clearing land mines in territories under its jurisdiction.

2008-11-23.title: In Battle Against Whaling, Groups Split on Strategy
2008-11-23.abstract: Greenpeace is distancing itself from the directly confrontational approach it once championed — and which the Sea Shepherd Conservation Society remains committed to.

2008-11-23.title: Superleague Formula
2008-11-23.abstract: Following up on the F2 post above…. This was what seemed to me a crazy idea more than half a decade ago. I was not surprised to see it fade out. But I am surprised now to see that it has not only faded back in again, but actually begun to happen. It’s a motor racing series in which the teams are like B-teams…but of worldwide soccer clubs. The cars are decked out in the colors of their parent soccer clubs. The drivers, however, are purebred racers.

2008-11-23.title: Bush Claims Modest Gains for Agenda in Peru Talks
2008-11-23.abstract: President Bush concluded his final meeting with Asia Pacific leaders with a collection of modest achievements.

2008-11-23.title: French Socialists Face Division and Derision After Vote for Leader
2008-11-23.abstract: A summer of embarrassing rivalries between Ségolène Royal and Martine Aubry has culminated in a narrow and disputed vote for the new party leader, Ms. Aubry.

2008-11-23.title: Pope Questions Interfaith Dialogue
2008-11-23.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI called for more discussion of the practical consequences of religious differences.

2008-11-24.title: Bangladesh Delays Vote
2008-11-24.abstract: The army-backed caretaker government of Bangladesh on Sunday postponed national elections by 11 days to assuage the concerns of one of the country’s two main political parties.

2008-11-24.title: Report Calls for Fresh Approach to Latin America
2008-11-24.abstract: A new report compiled by prominent former policy-makers recommends Washington overhaul its drug policies at home and pursue a rapprochement with Cuba.

2008-11-24.title: Sandinista Fervor Turns Sour for Former Comrades of Nicaragua’s President
2008-11-24.abstract: Former supporters of President Daniel Ortega of Nicaragua have accused him of rigging elections in an effort to spread his power, and lament the direction he is taking the country.

2008-11-24.title: Iraqi Foes of Security Deal Seek to Shield Assets
2008-11-24.abstract: Critics of the proposed security agreement say it can not, by itself, ensure that Iraq’s vast oil wealth and other assets would be protected against claims against the former government.

2008-11-24.title: As Taboos Ease, Saudi Girl Group Dares to Rock
2008-11-24.abstract: Saudi Arabia’s first all-girl rock band has an underground hit as the country’s harsh code of morals slowly thaws.

2008-11-25.title: Turkey’s Liberals Speaking Out as Reform Stalls
2008-11-25.abstract: Liberals have criticized Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan for what they call a shift away from his reformist ways and toward the military.

2008-11-25.title: Small Blast in Kosovo Chafes Bond With Germany
2008-11-25.abstract: Kosovar authorities arrested three Germans and accused them of involvement in an explosion this month in Pristina.

2008-11-25.title: A Fiery Czech Is Poised to Be the Face of Europe
2008-11-25.abstract: The Czech Republic is about to assume the rotating presidency of the E.U. and there is palpable fear that President Vaclav Klaus will embarrass the trading bloc.

2008-11-25.title: Slump May Limit Moves on Clean Energy
2008-11-25.abstract: A poor global economy and plunging prices for coal and oil are upending plans to curb the use of fossil fuels.

2008-11-25.title: Once Considered Invincible, Chávez Takes a Blow
2008-11-25.abstract: Across Venezuela, many of President Hugo Chávez’s supporters deserted him in regional elections.

2008-11-26.title: Study Cites Toll of AIDS Policy in South Africa
2008-11-26.abstract: Thabo Mbeki’s government could have prevented the premature deaths of 365,000 people, a new study says.

2008-11-26.title: Palestinian Forces Dilute Hebron’s Volatile Brew
2008-11-26.abstract: Hebron, the West Bank’s most explosive city, is undergoing a shake-up through the introduction of hundreds of Palestinian security officers.

2008-11-26.title: Ex-Diplomat Says Georgia Started War With Russia
2008-11-26.abstract: Erosi Kitsmarishvili, Tbilisi’s former ambassador to Moscow, told a parliamentary hearing in Georgia that Georgian authorities were responsible for starting the conflict with Russia.

2008-11-26.title: U.S. Presses NATO on Georgia and Ukraine
2008-11-26.abstract: The U.S. has started an unexpected diplomatic initiative, urging NATO allies to offer Georgia and Ukraine membership in the alliance without following the formal application process.

2008-11-26.title: To Soldiers, Bush Describes Wide Benefits From 2 Wars
2008-11-26.abstract: President Bush boasted of military successes in Afghanistan and Iraq and said he had helped bring democracy to both countries.

2008-11-27.title: Mistake Cited in Sinking of Boat by India
2008-11-27.abstract: A harrowing account suggests that the sinking of a Thai fishing trawler in the Gulf of Aden was a case of mistaken identity.

2008-11-27.title: A Night of Terror in Mumbai
2008-11-27.abstract: Mumbai suffered machine-gun and grenade assaults on at least two five-star hotels, the city’s largest train station, a movie theater and a hospital.

2008-11-27.title: Mexico’s Unsuccessful Drug War, Painfully Preserved and Hidden
2008-11-27.abstract: Run by the Mexican military and open only to graduating cadets and select guests, the Museo de los Enervantes presents the drug war in all its ugliness and complexity.

2008-11-27.title: Afghan Leader, Showing Impatience With War, Demands Timetable From NATO
2008-11-27.abstract: President Hamid Karzai bluntly rebuked NATO for its faltering campaign against the Taliban and Al Qaeda and demanded a timetable for the seven-year war to end.

2008-11-27.title: Effort Made to Save Man China Convicted of Spying
2008-11-27.abstract: His family and the U.S. try to save a Chinese man, Wo Weihan, from execution for spying.

2008-11-28.title: Under St. Paul’s Glow, Hints of Dark Times
2008-11-28.abstract: Many of the Americans who packed the Thanksgiving service at London’s St. Paul’s Cathedral have had their lives thrown into turmoil by the current financial crisis.

2008-11-28.title: Russian Missile Plan Gives a New European Trade Hub an Old Identity Crisis
2008-11-28.abstract: A proposal to once again make Kaliningrad a staging ground for missiles renews the old question: is the city Russia’s window, open to Europe, or a turret for firing on it?

2008-11-28.title: Venezuela and Russia Sign Accords
2008-11-28.abstract: While two countries signed accords intended to cooperate on areas like nuclear energy and oil exploration, there are no detailed timetables for bringing the deals to fruition.

2008-11-28.title: India’s Suspicion of Pakistan Clouds U.S. Strategy
2008-11-28.abstract: The attacks seem likely to hamper America’s project of promoting reconciliation between India and Pakistan.

2008-11-28.title: Canada’s Tories Seek to End Public Financing of Political Parties
2008-11-28.abstract: All three opposition parties in Parliament said they would vote against the measure, raising the possibility of a new federal election less than two months after the last one.

2008-11-29.title: Fighting in Mumbai Narrows
2008-11-29.abstract: The standoffs narrowed to a final battle, but the assault on the city continued to shake India and raise tensions with Pakistan.

2008-11-29.title: Crisis May Shift India’s Political Landscape
2008-11-29.abstract: Many expect the two-day siege of Mumbai to figure centrally in the coming national elections, with candidates making national security a priority.

2008-11-29.title: Wary Iraqis Weigh Deal That Shapes Their Future
2008-11-29.abstract: Iraqis are still trying to absorb what the newly-ratified security agreement that sets a 2011 withdrawal of American troops will mean — for the country and their own futures.

2008-11-29.title: Legal Hurdles in West Slow Pursuit of Pirates
2008-11-29.abstract: The bureaucratic and legal gears of Europe’s effort to respond to pirate attacks are proving to be not as swift as the hijackers, as Somali pirates captured another ship.

2008-11-29.title: Violence Clouds India’s Economic Future
2008-11-29.abstract: The terrorist siege is likely to threaten India’s already murky economic future and thwart plans to transform Mumbai into a regional financial center, economists said.

2008-11-30.title: On Thanksgiving: Chocolate Churches, an 8-Foot Tyrannosaurus and a Pineapple Alligator…
2008-11-30.abstract: Some unusual centerpieces adorned the Thanksgiving tables of U.S. service personnel in Iraq.

2008-11-30.title: V. P. Singh, a Leader of India Who Defended Poor, Dies at 77
2008-11-30.abstract: Mr. Singh was a former prime minister of India who was considered the father of coalition politics there and who stirred controversy by championing the rights of the country’s poorest citizens.

2008-11-30.title: Attacks Imperil Delicate U.S. Role Between Rivals
2008-11-30.abstract: American officials may have trouble preventing an Indian military response against Pakistan.

2008-11-30.title: Growing Security Concerns in West Africa
2008-11-30.abstract: Thousands of miles away from the battlefields of Iraq and Afghanistan, in the West African country of Mali, American soldiers are leading military exercises.

2008-11-30.title: Cabinet Minister Resigns in Wake of India Attacks
2008-11-30.abstract: The top domestic security official resigned in disgrace on Sunday for the failure to thwart or quickly contain the horrific terrorist attacks in Mumbai last week.
